info.title: CortexForge Chat & Observability API
info.description:
Main orchestration API for CortexForge with multi-project isolation.

    ## Authentication

    Most endpoints require JWT authentication via Bearer token in the Authorization header:

    ```
    Authorization: Bearer <your_jwt_token>
    ```

    To authenticate:
    1. Register: `POST /auth/register`
    2. Login: `POST /auth/login` - Returns access_token
    3. Use token in Authorization header for protected endpoints

    ## Role-Based Access Control

    - **Regular Users**: Can read all data (projects, conversations, messages, observability)
    - **Admins**: Full access including user management and deletion operations

    ## WebSocket Authentication

    WebSocket endpoints (Bridge) require token as query parameter:
    ```
    ws://localhost:28000/chat/bridge/ws/{project_id}?token=<your_jwt_token>
    ```

get.description:
Comprehensive health check endpoint
Returns status of all critical services with response times

post.description:
Create a new project with optional GitHub repo and local folder

Supports:
- Basic project creation in database
- Optional GitHub repository creation (requires GitHub PAT in user settings)
- Optional local folder creation with template structure
- Automatic git initialization and push to GitHub

Upload a screenshot/image to a project's screenshots folder.

Creates a 'screenshots' folder in the project's local_path if it doesn't exist.
Files are saved with timestamp prefixes for easy sorting.

Note: This endpoint uses form-data for the file upload.

get.description: List all screenshots in a project's screenshots folder.

post.description:
Upload a screenshot/image to a project's screenshots folder.

Creates a 'screenshots' folder in the project's local_path if it doesn't exist.
Files are saved with timestamp prefixes for easy sorting.

Supported formats: PNG, JPG, JPEG, GIF, WEBP
Max file size: 10MB

get.description: List all available scripts (requires authentication)

post.description:
Execute a script asynchronously (requires authentication)

Security features:
- Only authenticated users can execute scripts
- Only allowed scripts can be executed
- Commands are validated against allowlist with role-based permissions
- Arguments validated for injection attempts
- Scripts run with limited permissions
- Execution is logged and tracked with user context

get.description: List script execution history with optional filters (requires authentication)

schema.title: Response List Executions Scripts Executions Get

get.description: Get details of a specific script execution (requires authentication)

delete.description: Delete a script execution record (requires admin)

get.description:
Get CortexForge-related processes running on Windows host.
Proxies to Claude Code Bridge /processes endpoint.

Get logs from Docker containers

Fetches recent logs from specified Docker container(s).
Supports filtering by service and log level.

**Authentication Required**: Yes

**Parameters**:
- service: Service name or 'all' for all services
- level: Log level filter (all, debug, info, warn, error)
- limit: Maximum number of log lines per service (1-1000)

**Returns**: List of log entries with timestamps, service, level, message, and details

parameters.name: service

schema.description: Service to get logs from (all, cortexforge-postgres, etc.)
schema.title: Service

parameters.description: Service to get logs from (all, cortexforge-postgres, etc.)

schema.description: Log level filter (all, debug, info, warn, error)
schema.title: Level

parameters.description: Log level filter (all, debug, info, warn, error)

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Number of log lines per service
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Number of log lines per service

post.description:

Returns immediate subdirectories with quick project detection.
This is FAST (< 1 second) compared to deep scanning.

schema.title: Response Scan Directory Chat Import Scan Post

Scans multiple directories and returns all discovered projects.

schema.title: Response Get Import Candidates Chat Import Import Candidates Get

post.description:
Import a project into CortexForge

Creates project record in database and optionally applies standard structure.

Import multiple projects in one operation

Processes each project independently - failures don't stop other imports.

Initialize a project with AI-powered analysis and CortexForge standards

This endpoint:
1. **UNDERSTAND** - AI analyzes codebase to understand purpose and quality
2. **CREATE** - Generates missing CortexForge documentation and config
3. **MAP** - Links existing project elements to CortexForge standards
4. **INITIALIZE** - Sets up SDLC tracking and observability

The result is a fully CortexForge-managed project with:
- AI-generated documentation
- Gap tracking
- SDLC phase management
- Quality metrics
- Observability enabled

**Example Usage:**
```bash
POST /chat/projects/proj-abc123/initialize
{
  "force_recreate": false
}
```

**Returns:**
- Success status
- List of created files
- Identified gaps
- Quality assessment
- Recommended next steps

get.description:
Get AI-powered understanding of a project

This endpoint analyzes the project codebase and returns:
- Purpose and description
- Tech stack and architecture
- Quality metrics
- Security concerns
- Missing elements and recommendations

**Query Parameters:**
- `reanalyze`: Force re-analysis (default: false, uses cached if available)

**Example Usage:**
```bash
GET /chat/projects/proj-abc123/understanding
GET /chat/projects/proj-abc123/understanding?reanalyze=true
```

post.description:
Create CortexForge documentation structure for a project

This creates:
- .cortexforge/project.yaml
- docs/README.md
- docs/product/USER_GUIDE.md
- docs/product/GAPS_AND_ROADMAP.md
- docs/governance/SPEC_VS_REALITY.md
- docs/technical/ARCHITECTURE.md

**Query Parameters:**
- `force_recreate`: Overwrite existing files (default: false)

**Example Usage:**
```bash
POST /chat/projects/proj-abc123/create-structure?force_recreate=false
```

Submit a new task for asynchronous execution.

The task is queued in Redis and will be picked up by an available worker.
Returns immediately with task ID for status tracking.

get.description:
Get current status of a task.

Returns task metadata, progress, and current state.

Get task result once completed.

Returns the final result or error message for a completed task.

post.description:
Cancel a pending, queued, or running task.

Attempts to cancel the RQ job and marks the task as cancelled.

get.description:
List tasks with optional filtering.

Returns paginated list of tasks with filters for project, status, and type.

post.description:
Retry a failed task.

Creates a new RQ job for the task and increments retry count.

get.description:
Get detailed logs for a task.

Returns structured logs with timestamps, levels, and context.

post.description:
Clean up tasks that have been stuck (running/queued) for longer than timeout.

This endpoint marks old running tasks as failed to unblock the queue.
Requires admin access.

Process pending tasks that were blocked by concurrency limits.

This endpoint checks if blocked tasks can now be enqueued.
Requires admin access.

get.description: Get current concurrency status showing running task counts.

List Kanban cards for the current user, optionally filtered by project.

Cards are backed by Task rows with task_type='kanban_task'.
Archived cards are hidden by default - set include_archived=true to show them.

post.description:
Create a new Kanban card for a project.

This creates a Task row with type 'kanban_task' but does not enqueue any
background work yet. Automation can be wired later via the task queue.

patch.description: Update a Kanban card. Used for drag-and-drop column changes and edits.

delete.description: Delete a Kanban card (and its underlying Task record).

post.description:
Archive a completed Kanban card.

This moves the card to the "archived" status, hiding it from the default
board view while preserving it for historical records and learning.
Only cards in "done" status can be archived.
Restore an archived Kanban card back to the "done" status.

Manually trigger AI execution for a Kanban card.

This endpoint allows users to click a "Start Work" button to have Claude Code
CLI begin implementing the card's requirements. The card will be moved to
"in_progress" if not already there.

Retry execution for a failed Kanban card.

This endpoint allows users to retry a failed card. The execution history
is preserved to provide context for the retry attempt, which can help
the AI avoid repeating the same mistakes.

Use AI to analyze project goals and generate a backlog of Kanban cards.

This endpoint:
1. Reads project metadata (goals, description, target users)
2. Sends to AI with a prompt to break down into development tasks
3. Creates Kanban cards in "backlog" status

Returns the created cards.

get.description:
Detect Kanban cards that are stuck in "running" status.

Returns a list of stale cards with their status and recommended recovery action.
This helps identify cards where execution completed but the status wasn't updated.

post.description:
Recover all stale Kanban cards by moving them to appropriate statuses.

Recovery logic:
- Cards with ai_status="completed" -> move to manual_review
- Cards with ai_status="failed" -> move to backlog with error flag
- Cards with no execution -> reset to todo

Use dry_run=true to preview changes without applying them.

parameters.name: dry_run

schema.description: If true, only report what would be done without making changes
schema.title: Dry Run

parameters.description: If true, only report what would be done without making changes

post.description:
Recover a specific stale Kanban card.

Actions:
- auto: Determine based on ai_status (default)
- move_to_manual_review: Card completed, needs human review
- move_to_backlog_with_error: Card failed, move to backlog
- reset_to_todo: Reset completely for re-execution

parameters.name: action

schema.description: Recovery action: auto, move_to_manual_review, move_to_backlog_with_error, reset_to_todo
schema.title: Action

parameters.description: Recovery action: auto, move_to_manual_review, move_to_backlog_with_error, reset_to_todo

parameters.name: dry_run

schema.description: If true, only report what would be done
schema.title: Dry Run

parameters.description: If true, only report what would be done

Execute all quality checks for a phase

This endpoint triggers automated quality check execution.

Run complete automated workflow for a phase

This executes quality checks, validates gates, and updates phase status.
Execute a single quality check

get.description: Health check for SDLC API

Get WebSocket connection statistics.

**Permissions Required**: Authenticated user

**Returns**: Statistics about active connections, rooms, and users

post.description:
Register a new user account

Creates a new user with 'viewer' role by default.
Admin users can be created through the admin panel.

Authenticate user and return JWT tokens

Accepts username or email for login.
Returns both access token and refresh token.

Refresh access token using refresh token

Use this endpoint when access token expires.
Refresh tokens are valid for 7 days.

get.description:
Get current authenticated user information

Returns user profile data based on JWT token.

post.description:
Change current user's password

Requires current password for verification.

Reset a user's password (admin only)

Admins can reset any user's password without knowing the current password.

Logout current user

Clears authentication cookies and logs the logout event.

get.description:
Get user's API keys (masked for security)

Returns masked versions of API keys (e.g., "sk-ant-***...***1234")
and boolean flags indicating which keys are configured.

put.description:
Update user's API keys

Keys are encrypted before storage using Fernet encryption.
Pass empty string to remove a key, or omit to leave unchanged.

delete.description:
Delete all API keys for the current user

Use this to completely remove all stored API keys.

get.description: Health check endpoint

Search help articles.

Uses full-text search across titles, content, and keywords.
If no results found, the query is logged for documentation improvement.
Get a specific help article by slug.
Get all help categories.

schema.title: Response Get Categories Help Categories Get

post.description:
Submit a question that couldn't be answered.

This feeds into the self-improvement loop - questions are tracked
and used to identify documentation gaps.
Submit feedback on a help article.

get.description:
Get unanswered questions for documentation improvement.

Admin endpoint to see what questions users are asking that
don't have documentation yet.
Get help system statistics.
